evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Uses current stripe.PaymentIntent.create, stripe.Subscription.create with expand=['latest_invoice.payment_intent'], stripe.Webhook.construct_event - all consistent with current Stripe Python SDK patterns. Frontend uses PaymentElement and stripe.confirmPayment which are current. Internally consistent; no deprecated traps matched.
evidence.executability: All code blocks syntactically valid. Frontend uses REACT_APP_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY but .env.example lists STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY without the REACT_APP_ prefix - mismatch requires a fix. No sandbox-infeasible patterns (stripe CLI listen is acceptable for testing instruction).
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: (a) API key acquisition via Dashboard URL, (b) pip/npm install commands in bash, (c) .env.example block, (d) FastAPI backend code, (e) React frontend code, (f) test commands (stripe listen + test cards). Env var name mismatch between server .env (STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY) and frontend (REACT_APP_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY) - caps at 3 per rule.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Multiple official docs.stripe.com URLs placed near relevant code sections (Payment Element, Build subscriptions, Webhooks, Testing, Keys best practices). Official samples repo also cited. No fabricated URLs.

rubric_notes: Env var mismatch (REACT_APP_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY referenced in frontend but only STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY in .env.example) caps completeness at 3 per rubric; however playbook structurally provides all 6 pieces so adjusted to 3... revising: rule says cap at 3 on mismatch. Setting completeness to 3.
